coaching.title: Hill Repeats — 8 mi (Recovery Week)
coaching.message: Today is your vert session: 2 mi easy warm-up, then 10x 60-sec uphills walking down to recover, then 2 mi cool-down. This is recovery Week 9, so cap effort at 7/10 — form over fire. Drive from the hips, short quick steps, eyes up. On the steepest reps, practice the efficient power-hike you'll lean on across the Julian Alps' 2,500m of climbing. Let HR settle near the bottom of each rep; this week is about absorbing the build, not adding to it.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: An 8-mile hill session under ~90 min doesn't need heavy mid-run fueling — top off with 30-40g carbs 60-90 min before (banana + toast or a gel with water). If you push past 75 min, take 60g carbs in the back half. Within 30 min of finishing, hit ~25-30g protein plus carbs to refill glycogen. With travel and time-zone churn, keep electrolytes up across the day.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Hill reps load the calves, quads, and Achilles. Post-run, do 5-10 min easy walking before stopping, then foam-roll calves and quads and stretch hip flexors. It's a recovery week — protect sleep tonight (aim 7.5-8 hrs) and keep the rest of the day genuinely easy. No bonus mileage; let the legs bank the adaptation.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: Recovery weeks test discipline more than hard weeks test grit. Holding back at 7/10 when you feel strong IS the workout. Every controlled rep today is a rehearsal for the patience you'll need 13 weeks from now on the long climbs in Kranjska Gora — where the runners who hike smart and steady are the ones still moving well at km 40.
